_note: Swap / Tier-A-B / INT-availability diagnostics for the mage-scaling lane.

decks._note: DIAGNOSIS A: re-run the 3 FAILING boss1 direct-damage mages with a SMALL INT buff (statDeltas.int +3, i.e. realistic-INT 3 -> 6) to prove the scaling axis is the cause. elemMult would go 1.30 -> 1.75. If they now win, the failure is scaling, not deck quality.
decks.label: Mage Fire boss1 +INT3 (diag)

decks._note: DIAGNOSIS B: same 3 mages at boss1, baseline INT, but +STR3 instead (statDeltas.str). If STR fixes them but INT does not, that proves the elemMult curve (0.15) is structurally too weak vs strMult (0.25) AND that attack-category fire cards (Cinder-line) rely on STR a mage never has.
decks.label: Mage Fire boss1 +STR3 (diag)

decks._note: TIER A/B fire: baseline mid fire deck with a T2 direct burst (t2-agility-fire Flame Dart, magic) in slot vs the analogous T3 (t3-agility-fire-water Boilstep, magic). Both magic so both get elemMult. Decks otherwise identical.
decks.label: Fire TierA T2 (Flame Dart)

decks._note: SWAP TEST: fixed mid mage core; one flex slot. Baseline = t1-fire (Spark, weak filler). Candidate1 = t2-fire-fire Pyre (T2 detonator). Candidate2 = t3-agility-fire-water Boilstep (T3). Measures marginal value of each in identical context.
decks.label: Swap baseline (Spark)

decks._note: CATEGORY TEST: two equivalent mid fire decks, identical except the T3 finisher. Magic finisher (t3-air-fire-water Steaming Plague, gets elemMult) vs attack-category finisher (t3-attack-fire-fire Cinderlance, gets strMult=1.0 for mage, NO scaling). Shows attack-category 'mage' T3s are dead weight for a real mage.
decks.label: Cat magic finisher (Steaming Plague)

decks._note: WARRIOR EQUAL-PROGRESSION CONTROL at boss1 with NO T3 (pure T1/T2, matching a realistic loop-10 deck) to confirm the warrior beats doom_knight even without T3 power, isolating that the gap is the offense axis, not card tier access.
decks.label: Warrior physical boss1 (T1/T2 only)
